articles.title: Partnerships & Stakeholders: Science and Technology Community
articles.description: The role of global science, academia, research and innovation is enshrined in the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction where it is recognized for the catalytic, boundary-pushing and transformative potential it holds for addressing the dynamic drivers of disaster risk.

articles.title: UNDRR announces "Resilience Starts at Home" as the theme for the 2026 International Day for Disaster Risk Reduction
articles.description: The United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction (UNDRR) today announced that the theme for the 2026 International Day for Disaster Risk Reduction (IDDRR), observed on 13 October, will be “Resilience Starts at Home.”

articles.title: Advancing urban disaster resilience through global collaboration and South-South and Triangular Cooperation
articles.description: UNDRR, UNOSSC, PAHO, and UNTB held their 6th training in Apr-May 2026. The program boosted urban disaster resilience in developing and least developed countries by leveraging technological innovation, evidence-based decisions, and South-South cooperation.

articles.title: Building a safer society through science and technology: a compendium of Korea’s science and technology-based disaster management
articles.description: This casebook highlights the implementation of science and technology-based disaster management, featuring 24 Korean practices across four thematic areas, along with Korea's success factors and remaining challenges. It is available in English and Korean.

articles.title: The value of loss and damage data, from early warning to recovery
articles.description: From drought preparedness in Africa to institutional strengthening in Kyrgyzstan and post-conflict recovery planning in the agricultural sector in Lebanon, explore how loss and damage data is being used as a resource for decision-making.

articles.title: Tajikistan reviews progress on national early warning systems roadmap
articles.description: Tajikistan National Pillar Leads convened the second coordination meeting with relevant state agencies and international partners to review the implementation of the National Roadmap on Enhancing Early Warning Systems for 2024–2027.

articles.title: Asian Development Bank and UNDRR sign cooperation arrangement to strengthen disaster resilience in Asia and the Pacific
articles.description: ADB and UNDRR signed a Cooperation Arrangement to deepen joint efforts to strengthen disaster risk reduction in Asia-Pacific.

articles.title: Suriname advances toward a national early warning system that protects everyone, in time
articles.description: More than sixty national experts and international partners convened in Paramaribo on 5 and 6 May 2026, bringing all relevant actors together for the first time within the Early Warnings for All (EW4All) framework.

articles.title: Alexandria advances resilience planning through first technical workshop under MCR2030
articles.description: Alexandria is taking new steps to strengthen urban resilience and disaster risk reduction, as city counterparts and national partners joined an online technical workshop under the Making Cities Resilient 2030 (MCR2030) initiative.

articles.title: 24th Africa Working Group on Disaster Risk Reduction
articles.description: This outcome document of the 24th Africa Working Group on Disaster Risk Reduction.

articles.title: Programme Management Intern: Disaster Risk Reduction Financing & Risk Knowledge
articles.description: The intern will support the team in the administration and further development of work supporting the UNDRR Strategic Framework’s (2026-2030) catalytic areas on risk knowledge and financing for risk reduction.

articles.title: DRR Financing Observatory
articles.description: The Global Observatory for Early Warning System Investments is designed to share information about funding from multilateral development banks and funds supporting early warning system (EWS) to support building coherence, alignment, increased leveraging of EWS funding and understanding of funding gaps.

articles.title: Maximum Information
articles.description: Founded in 2020, Maximum Information is committed to increasing societal resilience to disasters through evolving risk modelling of natural hazards.

articles.title: Building business resilience in Venice: UNDRR and VSF put SMEs to the test at Venice Climate Week 2026
articles.description: UNDRR and the VSF brought disaster risk reduction to the heart of the business community, hosting Business Under Pressure — an interactive scenario exercise for small and medium-sized enterprises as part of Venice Climate Week 2026.
